Their reply was very positive and advised me that I would be accommodated.  My immediate impression of the facility upon arrival was very positive. The first person I interacted with couldn't have been more pleasant. She directed me to the emergency room where I was immediately registered as a traveling vet and escorted into a room for vitals and to await my visit with a doctor.  Both the triage nurse and the doctor were pleasant and accommodated my needs in a timely fashion. I was supplied with authorization to receive my insulin from the pharmacy. I then proceeded to pharmacy and was greeted by a friendly pharmacy tech who quickly expedited my prescription. In total, the entire time spent at the hospital spanned a whopping 30 minutes. In summary, I would Rate my experience with full stars and based on my experience, would recommend the Philadelphia VA to any traveling vet with similar needs as mine. Thank you, thank you thank you dr's and staff of the Philadelphia VA for making my experience so pleasant."""]

[ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURaLU4tQ1lREAE; reviewBody; """I saw the reviews here and took a chance at staying here, and I entirely understand the wide spectrum in terms of reviewsI'll start by repeating what I've said to numerous people, including employees, family, and friends: you will only be treated with respect if you are coherent, calm, and complacent. All qualities not generally found in a place meant for emergency treatment for mental illnessI suppose I was lucky, with my situation coinciding with those three qualities. I won't discredit anyone's experience here, positive or otherwise, but I personally would not recommend Friends Hospital if you need genuinely anything more than a place to stay away from your current situation and are capable enough to keep yourself safe to a certain degreeWhile I was there, I experienced both ends of the human compassion spectrum, from a technician helping me contact a family member daily, to being flatly ignored when asking for items (such as my daily hygiene bag in the evenings) multiple times. I also experienced medications being given to me that were not prescribed by the psychiatrist, and witnessed emotional and verbal (sometimes physical) abuse between patients and staff alike. I am aware that mental health services as a career is draining and taxing, but I also believe that the career choice should not be taken lightly, especially by those who actively complain that other places offer more money for better hoursIn the hallway, there's a poster that details what a usual day looks like in terms of activities, all on a weekly cycle. Oftentimes, this schedule was not followed. An hour of outdoors time was not offered every day as it should have been, much less actually provided. If we did have groups, they were not done as often as it was supposed to, and the technician-led groups just did not happen. There were two choice instances where the technician-led group time slots were used, but both times were led by patients insteadThe only truly kind and friendly people I encountered were those who did not deal with patients on a constant basis, such as nurses, medical students, social workers, and admin staff. If it weren't for them, I might've done something drastic when I got homeIn all, this was absolutely not the best facility I've stayed at, and I would not recommend it to anyone who isn't excellent at pretending they are fine when at the absolute end of their rope. If you are angry, upset, confused, or anything besides calm and mildly sad, you will be met with disrespect and borderline contempt by the general staff. I truly hope that the hospital takes any and all complaints seriously, as the potential for a healing environment is there, it's just hidden under deep layers of apathy and dismissal."""]

[Crisis Response Center Adults (Temple Health - Episcopal Campus); text; "The CRC is a 24-hour psychiatric emergency service where individuals experiencing behavioral health crises receive evaluation, treatment and referral for additional care. We treat episodes of psychosis, intoxication, substance withdrawal, and threatened or attempted suicide, among other conditions.Patients can receive the following services: - Evaluation & stabilization- Medical treatment when necessary- Medication to calm or stabilize them, as necessary- Access to our six-bed, 23-hour observation unit for those who require more time for assessment Our goal is to refer patients to the most appropriate services after discharge, which may include: - The inpatient Behavioral Health Center onsite at Episcopal Campus- Other agencies and facilities throughout the Delaware Valley- An inpatient substance use program- Outpatient care- Homeless shelters or recovery housesThe CRC accepts walk-in patients experiencing a behavioral health crisis, or they may receive treatment at the center at the direction of medical providers or law enforcement authorities. Patients may access the CRC by visiting the Episcopal Campus emergency room. Upon arrival at the CRC, patients undergo an initial interview with a nurse or mental health technician, followed by a second, more detailed discussion with an attending psychiatrist or psychiatry resident. Temple Health's Episcopal Campus accepts Medicaid and Medicare, and offers Financial Assistance & Charity Care (free or reduced-priced care) to uninsured and/or low-income persons who qualify."]

[Family First (Child Guidance Resource Centers); text; "Family First believes that the most effective way to help troubled children and adolescents is through a family-focused, home-based model designed to recognize struggles and build on family strengths. Services:- Family therapy- Individual counseling- Parent education- Case management- Family support- 24-hour on-call supportFamily First is a 32 week process. Your family is assigned a team of two Master’s level clinicians who work closely with you in the home, school and community to address not only your child’s emotional struggles but the larger systemic issues contributing to troublesome behavior. The flexibility of being in the home and community allows your therapists to address the root of your struggles and create long-term positive change.Medical Assistance (Medicaid) is accepted for these services."]

[Behavior Health Rehabilitation Services (Philadelphia Mental Health Center); text; "Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services (BHRS) is a service that focuses on the behavioral health needs of a child in his/her everyday environment. BHRS is client-centered yet family-focused, identifies the needs of the child in the home, school and community and develops a program which “wraps around” the child. Services provided include:- Weekly scheduled sessions with clinical team members in all settings relevant to their child- Clinical treatment by a well-rounded, certified team of psychologists and clinicians- A Service Specialist who can address any concerns that ariseBHRS is funded through Pennsylvania State Medical Assistance (PAMA), regardless of family income. BHRS is not exclusive, and can often be prescribed in conjunction with other behavioral health and intellectual disability services.To request our evaluation services, you can contact us by phone or fill out our on-line “intake” form. Office visits will be required to assess both the initial and ongoing needs of the child. These office visits average four times per year."]
[Adult Inpatient Mental Health Services (Belmont Behavioral Health System); text; "At Belmont Behavioral Health Hospital, we are dedicated to providing the highest quality of care to adults age 18 and older who are seeking help for mental health challenges. We offer several levels of adult inpatient programs at our facility in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.We offer:- Inpatient psychiatric care- Dual diagnosis of a substance use- Mood and personality disorder treatmentWhen you or someone you love comes to Belmont for help with a mental health crisis, our expert team will conduct a comprehensive assessment to determine which adult inpatient program is the best level of care to meet your unique needs. We provide adults with the individualized care that helps them heal and recover in the way that is most beneficial for them.Belmont Behavioral Hospital accepts Medicare & Medicaid."]

[Children and Adolescent Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services (WES Health System); text; "Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services, also known as Wraparound, are services for children and adolescents who may be experiencing mental and behavioral health challenges in their daily lives. Wraparound services are a shift from traditional mental health services provided in a therapist’s office, to services where the child normally is, in the home, school, or community. This program provides:- Wraparound services - building constructive relationships and support networks- ReferralMedicaid and Medicare accepted. Private pay available.  Obtaining BHRS services first requires an evaluation by a psychologist. The request is then submitted to CBH (Community Behavioral Health) for an approval. Given the child’s diagnosis, services can be approved for 6 months – 1 year, at a time."]

These supports add structure while parents go back to school for a better degree, navigate the start of a career,  establish next steps in housing, and step into a homebuying pipeline to secure generational wealth for their family."]

[Community Integrated Recovery Center CIRC at Penrose (Citizens Acting Together Can Help, Inc.); text; """Using clinical protocols and activities that promote the concepts of personal strengths and resiliency, these programs combine outpatient psychiatric services with community-oriented psychiatric rehabilitation servicesMembers work on skills which will help them achieve a variety of goals ranging from psychiatric symptom reduction to independent living and vocational/educational placement. Services are provided on-site and in a community-based setting. This program works with a forensic population that requires many additional skills as they attempt to move away from an institutional lifestyle."""]

[Developmental Behavioral Pediatrics (Nemours Children's Health System); text; "Developmental behavioral pediatrics is a specialty that focuses on children’s growth and development. Nemours Children's Health System works together with a team of medical specialists to develop individualized treatment plans for kids with developmental delays, developmental disabilities or medical conditions that affect their learning and behavior.This program provides: - A comprehensive evaluation- A physical exam, checking things like coordination, reflexes and muscle tone- Observations of how your child communicates, moves and plays- Further testing, if needed, to evaluate concerns such as speech and language abilities, fine motor skills, gross motor skillsNemours Children's Health System may also provide a full report of its findings and, if a problem is diagnosed, specific recommendations (such as at-home or school-based therapies, early intervention services, counseling and/or medications, guidance and referrals about agencies and educational programs that may be helpful).Nemours Children's Health System primarily focuses on children under the age of 7 years old in this specialty. They have a limited number of appointments for older children and teens. They prioritize appointments for young children, but please know that there can be a significant wait — potentially six months or more — for appointments for children of any age.Nemours Children's Health System accepts Medicaid and Medicare, and offers financial assistance to eligible uninsured families. Please call 800-416-4441 if you are in the Delaware/Greater Philadelphia area and call 407-650-7715 if you are in Central Florida."]

[Create (Child Guidance Resource Centers); text; "CREATE is a year round, innovative group outpatient service for children and young adults with a diagnosis of autism. Participants will build social and communication skills, improve problem solving and emotional regulation, and enhance flexibility and motivation. Each day in CREATE, every child will participate in evidence-based interactive therapies including:- ABA (Applied Behavior Analysis)- PRT (Pivotal Response Therapy)- Hidden Curriculum- QuestTM- Social ThinkingTM- social stories- behavior mappingFamily therapy and parent workshops are also a component of CREATE. CREATE is an outpatient service, so anyone can make refer someone. Parents, teachers, pediatricians, grandparents, clinicians, case managers, anyone can make a referral, even clients themselves!CREATE is covered by Medical Assistance and most private insurance plans. In most cases, transportation is provided."]

[Addiction Medicine And Health Advocates (Addiction Medicine and Health Advocates Inc); text; "Addiction Medicine and Health Advocates, Inc. Methadone Maintenance is provided on both levels of care.Services provided include: Assessment and intake Medication-assisted treatment (methadone maintenance) Individual counseling & psychotherapy Group psychotherapy Psycho education Medical & psychiatric evaluations  Aftercare & follow up services  Referrals (educational, medical, vocational, psychiatric, higher levels of care) Couples & family therapy Drug screening To begin the admissions process, please contact us. You will be asked to complete a short screening interview and if initial eligibility criteria is met, you will be scheduled for an assessment.At the time of your assessment, you will need to bring: Current, state-issued photo ID Insurance cardAMHA is a private not-for-profit organization dependent upon patient fees for its continuance.
